source_title: FDA Warning Letter 320-25-91 to Medical Chemical Corporation (MARCS-CMS 706007)
document_type: FDA CGMP warning letter to an OTC drug manufacturer citing multiple violations (component identity testing, process control, quality-unit oversight) plus unapproved-drug and listing violations
primary_pressure: Systemic quality-system failure vs vendor/supplier fault vs a single bad decision, with off-axis regulatory-status violations. Three independent CGMP pillars support the systemic reading.
notes: Winner (systemic quality) survives single-row removal but weakens on the supplier axis if the 'firm is responsible despite supplier COAs' bridge is removed. Fresh letter, not the AACE letter used in the prior batch.
